taxonomy: Hieracium brachybrachion is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is classified as a member of the genus Hieracium, which includes over 600 species of plants commonly known as hawkweeds. The genus Hieracium is a large and diverse group of plants that are native to many parts of the world, including Europe, Asia, and North America. Hieracium brachybrachion is a specific species within this genus, and its classification is further refined by its membership in the subgenus Pilosella. The exact classification of Hieracium brachybrachion may also be further detailed through its inclusion in specific sections or series within the subgenus Pilosella.

intro: Hieracium brachybrachion, also known as dwarf hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium brachybrachion include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made from Hieracium brachybrachion include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and skin problems.
